decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to enhance lateral heat spreading and reduce thermal resistance. A conservative 40% increase is proposed, staying well within the allowed 2x step ratio.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity improves lateral heat distribution away from the chip hotspot, directly lowering the peak temperature.

expected_effects: Improved thermal gradient across the spreader layer
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate or boundary conditions.
High conductivity materials may be cost-prohibitive in real-world applications, though this is a simulation.
